title: Kylie Leuluai

[[Thomas Leuluai]] (cousin)
[[Macgraff Leuluai]] (cousin)
[[Marley Leuluai]] (son) }} }} '''Kylie Leuluai''' (born 29 March 1978) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] and Samoa international representative {{rlp|pr}}, he most notably played at club level for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. He also played club football in Australia for [[National Rugby League]] clubs; the [[Balmain Tigers]], [[Wests Tigers]], [[Sydney Roosters]], [[Parramatta Eels]], and the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]. Leuluai was a massive part of the Leeds Rhinos success during his nine seasons at the club, winning a total of six [[Super League Grand Final|Grand Finals]], two [[World Club Challenge]]s and two [[Challenge Cup]]s in that time to make him the most successful overseas player in the club's history.{{cite news|url= http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=81326&includeref=dynamic|work= [[Leeds Rhinos Official Website]]|title=Player Biography Kylie Leuluai|first=Ronnie|last=Rhino|date=1 January 2015}} ==Background== Leuluai was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He attended the Church College of New Zealand in Hamilton, New Zealand, playing both Rugby union and league. ==Playing career== Leuluai played for [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]], [[Wests Tigers]], [[Sydney Roosters]], [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], and Manly in the [[NRL]]. In 2007, he joined [[Super League]] club [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. In 2007 Leuluai played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2007 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. The [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club defeated the [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] 33–6. In 2008 Leuluai played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2008 World Club Challenge]] against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. The [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] side defeated [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] 18–10. Later that year, Leeds once again made it to the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. They defeated [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] 24–16. In 2009, Leuluai played for the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2009 World Club Challenge]] against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]. The Leeds side were defeated by Manly 28–20. He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009}}{{cite news|last=AAP|author-link=Australian Associated Press|title=Leeds claim third successive Grand Final|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57077/Default.aspx|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[National Rugby League|nrl]]|date=11 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121003225647/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57077/Default.aspx|archive-date=3 October 2012|url-status=dead}}{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}} In 2010, Leuluai played for Leeds in the [[2010 World Club Challenge]] against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. The Leeds side were defeated by Melbourne 18–10. It was later found that Melbourne were in serious breach of the NRL Salary Cap Rules and they had the Title stripped from them. It is well known, yet unofficial that this action automatically made the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] outfit World Club Champions of 2010. [[File:Kylie Leuliai.jpg|thumb|left|Leuluai playing for Leeds]] He played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live}} In June 2011, Leuluai announced he will retire at the end of the 2012 season, signing a new one-year contract with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the process. Later that year he played at {{rlp|pr}} for Leeds in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}{{cite news|last=Brown|first=Oliver|title=Challenge Cup final: Leeds Rhinos 18 Wigan Warriors 28|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8727501/Challenge-Cup-final-Leeds-Rhinos-18-Wigan-Warriors-28.html|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=27 August 2011}} He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=The Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}} Towards the end of 2011, Leuluai announced he would play on into 2012, but would retire at the end of the 2013 [[Super League]] Season. In 2012 Leuluai played for the Leeds in the [[2012 World Club Challenge]] against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]. Leeds beat Manly 26–12. He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}} Leeds went on to win the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] with victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}{{cite web |date=6 October 2012 |title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final |access-date=20 November 2019 |work=The Guardian}} Towards the end of 2012, Leuluai announced he would play on into 2013, but would retire at the end of the 2014 [[Super League]] Season. In 2013 Leuluai played for Leeds in the [[2013 World Club Challenge]] against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. Leeds were defeated by Melbourne 18–14. Leuluai once again halted his retirement plans by announcing he would play on into the 2014 [[Super League]] Season, but would hang up his boots at the end of 2014. In 2014, Leuluai played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}} Surprisingly enough, Leuluai halted his retirement plans yet again and announced he would play on into the 2015 [[Super League]] Season, but would most definitely hang up his boots at the end of 2015 along with fellow [[Rugby league positions#Prop|Prop Forward]] [[Jamie Peacock]] MBE. In June 2015, Leuluai once again announced his intention to retire at the end of 2015,{{cite news|url= http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-prop-leuluai-confirms-retirement-at-the-end-of-the-season-1-7298738|work= [[Yorkshire Evening Post]]|title=Leeds Rhinos: Prop Leuluai confirms retirement at the end of the season|first=Peter|last=Smith|date=9 June 2015}} saying that he felt it was time to move on to a new challenge. Leuluai expected to be staying on and working with the club on a part-time basis while studying at university as well. He played in the [[2015 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/29/leeds-emphatically-shut-out-hull-kr-to-win-challenge-cup|title=Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup|work=The Guardian|date=29 August 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}{{cite news |title=Scoreboard |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=2982 |date=31 August 2015 |page=31}}{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34086760 |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 0-50 Leeds Rhinos |work=BBC Sport |date=29 August 2015 |access-date=29 August 2015}} On 3 July 2015, Leuluai scored a try on his 250th appearance for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33330118|title = Leeds 46-18 St Helens|work = BBC Sport}} against reigning Super League champions St Helens as Leeds completed the double with a ruthless 46–18 victory to go three points clear of them with just three games left before the start of the Super 8s. Leuluai would finish his career with a sixth Grand Final victory with Leeds after defeating Wigan in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final|2015 Grand Final]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=7 November 2019}} ==Representative career== Leuluai played for [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] in 1998 and at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].''New Zealand Rugby League Annual '98'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1998. p.118 Leuluai was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup squad|Samoa training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750 |title=Samoa name World Cup Squad |publisher=League Unlimited |date=5 August 2008 |access-date=6 August 2008 |archive-date=17 July 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750 |url-status=dead }} In 2009 he was named as part of the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] side for the [[2009 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]].[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091010154654/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php |date=10 October 2009 }} ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009 He was named [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] player of the year for 2009.{{cite news|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992 |title=Hayne named Player of the Year |newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|date=10 November 2009 |access-date=19 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110720050935/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992 |archive-date=20 July 2011 |url-status=live }} ===Rugby League International Origin Match=== Kylie Leuluai was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin]] match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] on 10 June 2011.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}} ==Honours== ===Club=== * [[Super League]] (6): [[Super League XII|2007]], [[Super League XIII|2008]], [[Super League XIV|2009]], [[Super League XVI|2011]], [[Super League XVII|2012]], [[Super League XX|2015]] * [[World Club Challenge]] (2): [[2008 World Club Challenge|2008]], [[2012 World Club Challenge|2012]] * [[League Leader's Shield]] (2): [[Super League XIV|2009]], [[Super League XX|2015]] * [[Challenge Cup]] (2): [[Challenge Cup|2014, 2015]] ==Personal life== He is the father of [[Burnley FC]] defender [[Marley Leuluai]]. [[James Leuluai]] is his uncle and his cousins are [[Thomas Leuluai]] and [[Macgraff Leuluai]]. Kylie belongs to [[the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints]] and is renowned for being able to bench press 225 kg – the heaviest weight lifted by an NRL player.{{cite news|url= http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-eagle-rock/story-e6frexnr-1111112212419|work= [[The Daily Telegraph]]|title=The Eagle Rock|first=James|last=Hooper|date=15 September 2006}} ==Career highlights and honours== *''Junior Club:'' [[Papatoetoe Panthers]] *[[Wests Tigers]] *[[Sydney Roosters]] *[[Parramatta Eels]] *[[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] 2004–06 *''[[Super League]] Championship:'' member of the 2007 Championship [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team *''[[Super League]] Championship:'' member of the 2008 Championship [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team *''[[Super League]] Championship:'' member of the 2009 Championship [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team *''[[Super League]] Championship:'' member of the 2011 Championship [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team *''[[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]]:'' member of the 2012 Winning Team against [[England Rugby League]] *''[[Super League]] Championship:'' member of the 2012 Championship [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team *''[[Challenge Cup]] Winners:'' member of the 2014 Challenge Cup Winners [[Leeds Rhinos]] Team ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/rhinos-profiles/Rhinos-squad-Kylie-Leuluai.2046534.jp Rhinos squad: Kylie Leuluai] *[https://warringtonwolves.com/performance-team/ Warrington Wolves profile] {{Navboxes |title= Leeds Rhinos squads | bg=#000080 | fg=#FFE303 | bordercolor=#FFE303 |list1= {{Leeds Rhinos -
